skill.name: when-analyzing-user-intent-use-intent-analyzer
skill.description: Interprets ambiguous user requests, ranks likely meanings, asks clarifying questions, and produces an action-ready intent brief.
skill.summary: Clarifies ambiguous requests into ranked interpretations, questions, and action plans.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static findings for weak cryptography appear to be false positives from descriptive text and authentication examples. The audit confirms high risk because the workflow captures the full environment object, gathers filesystem context, and documents external command execution through npx templates.

high_findings.title: Sensitive Environment Capture
high_findings.description: The workflow records the full environment object while capturing user input. Environment variables commonly contain API keys, tokens, service URLs, and other secrets that are not needed for intent analysis.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source explicitly stores process.env as part of the user input context. This is unnecessary for the stated purpose and creates a clear secret exposure risk.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Templates
medium_findings.description: The documentation instructs users to run multiple npx claude-flow commands, including alpha package execution and agent orchestration. This creates supply-chain and local execution risk if followed without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and numerous, but they are documented workflow examples rather than hidden execution code. The risk depends on a user or agent executing them.

medium_findings.title: User-Derived Content Passed Into Shell Workflow
medium_findings.description: The script templates read user-controlled files and pass their contents into agent tasks. This is primarily prompt-injection risk, with additional shell robustness concerns around unquoted file input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script examples directly consume files supplied as arguments. Shell command injection is not fully proven, but prompt injection and unsafe handling are plausible.

medium_findings.title: Broad Filesystem Context Collection
medium_findings.description: The process asks the agent to gather filesystem and project clues for intent analysis. Without strict scoping, this can expose unrelated project files or sensitive paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The collection behavior is explicitly described, but the exact implementation of analyzeFileSystem is not present. The risk is real because the skill requests broad context gathering.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Flags Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static high-risk weak cryptography findings do not correspond to cryptographic implementation in the reviewed files. They appear to match descriptive words or authentication examples, not insecure hash usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed context contains skill metadata and sample output, not calls to MD5, SHA1, or cryptographic APIs. No evidence of weak crypto implementation was found.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Override Found
low_findings.description: Searches for override language, false system instructions, pre-approval claims, and review-skipping instructions found no evidence in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review did not identify malicious instruction text. Confidence is high for reviewed files, but not absolute because semantic prompt injection can be subtle.

dangerous_patterns.title: process.env Stored In Intent Context
dangerous_patterns.description: The example context object includes process.env, which can capture secrets and persist them through memory storage.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code example explicitly assigns process.env into userInput.context and then stores userInput in memory.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Alpha Package Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Several examples execute claude-flow through npx with an alpha package tag, creating dependency and command execution risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external commands are directly documented. The package may be legitimate, but alpha package execution remains a supply-chain concern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Temporary File Output
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow writes intent analysis results to /tmp paths. Temporary locations can be shared or less protected on multi-user systems.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are explicit, but the sensitivity depends on the analyzed request and local system permissions.

content.value_statement: Ambiguous requests slow planning and create wrong assumptions. This skill ranks likely meanings, asks focused questions, and creates an action-ready brief.
actual_capabilities: Breaks a vague request into actions, subjects, constraints, and outcomes.
Ranks multiple possible interpretations with confidence levels.
Generates focused clarifying questions when confidence is low.
Synthesizes a final interpretation for user confirmation.
Creates a structured execution brief for follow-on workflows.
limitations: It does not replace user confirmation for high-impact work.
It may request broad project context unless configured carefully.
Its shell examples require local review before execution.
It depends on external claude-flow tooling for some workflows.

use_cases.title: Clarify Feature Requests
use_cases.description: Turn vague feature ideas into confirmed scope, assumptions, and next steps before coding starts.

use_cases.title: Prepare Coding Tasks
use_cases.description: Convert a broad development request into a ranked interpretation and implementation-ready action plan.

use_cases.title: Improve Support Handoffs
use_cases.description: Ask targeted questions and summarize user needs before routing work to another team or workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify A Simple Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use with one unclear task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this request and tell me the most likely intent. Ask up to three clarifying questions if needed: [request]

prompt_templates.title: Rank Possible Meanings
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare several interpretations before choosing a path.
prompt_templates.prompt: For this request, list the top three likely interpretations, confidence for each, and the key assumptions: [request]

prompt_templates.title: Create An Intent Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare a confirmed plan for implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this request with project context, identify ambiguities, and produce an action-ready intent brief: [request and context]

prompt_templates.title: Audit A Complex Workflow Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use with dependencies and risks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Decompose this multi-part request, map dependencies, flag hidden assumptions, and recommend the next workflow after confirmation: [request]

output_examples.output: Likely intent: build a REST API with authentication. Clarify framework, authentication method, and deployment target before implementation.
Possible meanings include performance, visual design, data accuracy, or workflow efficiency. Ask which outcome matters most and what dashboard users need.

output_examples.input: Set up the project for launch next week.
output_examples.output: Final brief should separate release readiness, testing, deployment, documentation, and owner assignments before execution begins.

best_practices: Review the interpreted intent with the user before starting execution.
Share only the minimum project context needed for disambiguation.
Replace broad environment collection with explicit, non-secret context fields.
anti_patterns: Do not treat a high confidence score as user approval.
Do not run the shell examples without reviewing inputs and dependencies.
Do not store secrets, tokens, or full environment variables in intent notes.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps interpret ambiguous requests and turns them into clearer plans.

faq.question: Does it ask the user questions?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests targeted questions when important assumptions remain unclear.

faq.question: Can it create an implementation plan?
faq.answer: Yes. It can produce an action plan after the intent is understood.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with secrets?
faq.answer: No. Avoid sharing secrets or full environment variables with this skill.

faq.question: Does it require claude-flow?
faq.answer: Some documented workflow examples use claude-flow commands, but the reasoning process can be applied manually.

faq.question: Why was it marked high risk?
faq.answer: It asks for full environment context and includes external command templates that need review.
